name: Christmas Cake (British)
recipeInstructions:
(Note: (1) The mixed dried fruit should be such things as seedless raisins and currants. (2) The 1/4 teaspoon of "flavoring" can be almond, or lemon, or vanilla, or a mixture of the three. (3) "Mixed spice" is a combination of cinnamon, allspice and mace: about 1/3 of each.)
1. Find a 9-inch cake pan at least three inches deep; grease it, and line the bottom and sides with a double thickness of greased baking parchment.
2. Tie folded brown paper or newspaper around the outside of the pan (the paper is insulation to keep the sides of the cake from scorching as it bakes).
3. Set oven to 320-300°F; have folded paper ready to stand the pan on when baking—place low in oven.
4. Pick over dried fruit, removing any stems or debris. Wash, drain well, and spread over a cloth on a tray to dry until completely dry.
5. Put dried fruit into a bowl and pour a small wine-glass of spirit over it. Cover well and leave overnight.
6. The next day, add chopped peel, blanched chopped almonds, and minced cherries to the dried fruit in the bowl; mix thoroughly.
7. Sift flour, salt, bicarbonate of soda, and mixed spice together.
8. Beat eggs well with molasses and flavoring until combined.
9. Add butter into a warmed large bowl and beat until soft. Then add sugar and beat until light and creamy.
10. Beat in the egg mixture about one-sixth at a time; fold in half the flour mixture.
11. Add remaining flour to fruit mixture and mix well.
12. Stir in the fruit and creamed mixture, then add into pan.
13. Bake for 3½ to 4 hours or until done. If top browns too fast, cover with few thicknesses of paper; reduce oven heat slightly every hour by about 10 degrees.
14. Test with a skewer for doneness when finished baking.
15. Keep the wrapped cake in place until completely cooled. After cooling, invert the cake and lift parchment from bottom.
16. Sprinkle the cake generously with spirit of choice; wrap well and put into sealed tin.
17. Store in a cool place (not necessarily refrigerated). The cake benefits from occasional unwrapping and sprinkling with more spirit; it takes about 2 months to achieve maximum mellowness.
